records.ety: [OE. wroth, wrap, AS. wrāð wroth, crooked, bad; akin to wrīðan to writhe, and to OS. wrēðangry, D. wreed cruel, OHG. reid twisted, Icel. reiðr angry, Dan. & Sw. vred. See Writhe, and cf. Wrath.]

defs: Full of wrath; angry; incensed; much exasperated; wrathful.
quotes: Revel and truth as in a low degree, They be full wroth [i. e., at enmity] all day.
Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.
